title: Act II — Kitchen, Bath, Stairs

references.@Image1: character sheet — subject appearance, wardrobe, posh British demeanor
references.@Image2: style plate — American manor, champagne grade, 50mm lens, tungsten light
references.@Image3: Chunk 2 storyboard grid — room layouts and compositions

prompt:
@Image1's character as the subject, gold floral wrap dress throughout. She speaks throughout with a posh British accent — flat monotone, matter-of-fact, no inflection. Scene and color grade reference @Image2. Shot sequence reference @Image3. Warm prestige American manor, cream walls honey hardwood oil portraits brass lamps soft tungsten light, 50mm shallow depth of field warm champagne grade, deadpan blocking sincere camera, 13 seconds.

0–4s: Wide manor kitchen cream cabinetry copper pots honey hardwood brass pendant, young chef kneels weeping over single asparagus spear on cutting board, she foreground stirs tea in china cup talks to camera with British deadpan never looks at chef, cut to close-up 50mm static deadpan face gold hoops blurred weeping chef behind, quiet kitchen faint sob tea spoon clink.

4–8s: Wide primary bathroom cream walls gilt mirror white marble vanity brass sconces, she seated reapplies red lipstick, solicitor in grey suit stands in honey-hardwood hall doorway behind on phone call she does not acknowledge, mirror reflection medium then tight vanity close-up deadpan British eyes, muffled phone distant dialogue uninterrupted.

8–13s: Wide top grand curved staircase cream walls oil portraits gold frames honey hardwood banister brass chandelier, she descends gold heels, Black butler follows carrying white marble classical bust wearing gold Venetian carnival mask on bust face, she keeps talking to camera with posh British accent never looks back, medium track down low angle on landing, heel clicks on hardwood.

Voice: posh British accent, British female, flat monotone, fast continuous delivery.

Dialogue: My housekeeper thinks dust is a suggestion. I fired a chef for emoting at asparagus. He wept. I offered a tissue. It was linen. Wrong weight. In the bath I told my ex's solicitor I don't do group chats. He does. That's why he's an ex's solicitor. On the stairs I remembered I still owe Venice an apology. I won't pay it. Venice was smug.

style.mood: deadpan blocking, surreal background details treated as normal
style.pacing: hard cuts between rooms every 3–4 seconds
style.accent: posh British accent throughout, flat monotone, upper-class cadence

In the bath I told my ex's solicitor I don't do group chats. He does. That's why he's an ex's solicitor. On the stairs I remembered I still owe Venice an apology. I won't pay it. Venice was smug.
